Pt is a 89 year old female with PMH of HTN, hyperlipidemia, IBS, OAB, and hypothyroidism who presents for follow
up. Medications were reviewed and reconciled. Taking as prescribed with no adverse reactions. Feeling well. She
c/o fatigue, but is otherwise, doing well. No other complaints.
Patient reports fatigue but reports no fever, no chills, no night sweats, and no significant weight gain/loss. She
reports constipation but reports no abdominal pain, no nausea, no vomiting, no heartburn, no bloating, and
no difficulty swallowing. She reports increased frequency, leaking urine, and incontinence but reports no
pain during urination, no urgency, no blood in urine, and no flank pain. She reports dizziness but reports no
vertigo, no loss of consciousness, no memory loss, no numbness, no tingling, no headaches, and no seizures;
aphasia. She reports anxiety but reports no depression and no sleep disturbance. She reports no eye
irritation and no vision change. She reports no chest pain and no rapid or irregular heartbeat. She reports no
shortness of breath, no wheezing, and no cough. She reports no vaginal lesions, no vaginal discharge, no
vaginal itching or burning, and no vaginal odor. She reports menopause. She reports no muscle aches, no
muscle cramps, no muscle weakness, no joint pains, and no back pain. She reports no rash, no skin ulcers, no
abnormal moles, and no nail discoloration. She reports no breast lumps, no breast pain, and no nipple
discharge. She reports no heat/cold intolerance, no increased thirst, no abnormal hair loss/growth, and no
change in libido. She reports no swollen glands, no bruising, and no bleeding disorder.
Constitutional: General Appearance: well-nourished and well-developed. Level of Distress: NAD. Ambulation:
Eyes: Lids and Conjunctivae: no discharge or pallor and non-injected. Pupils: PERRLA. EOM: EOMI. Sclerae: non-
icteric. Vision: peripheral vision grossly intact and acuity grossly intact.
Ear, Nose, Mouth, and Throat: Ears: no lesions on external ear and normal hearing. Nose: no lesions on
external nose or nasal discharge. Mouth: no lip lesions/ulcers.
Neck: Neck: supple, trachea midline, and no masses. Thyroid: no enlargement.
Repiratory: Effort: no dyspnea. Auscultation: clear to auscultation.
Cardiovascular: Neck Vessels: no carotid bruits. Pulses: normal throughout. Extremities: no cyanosis, edema,
Gastrointestinal: Bowel Sounds: normal bowel sounds. Inspection and Palpation: no masses, tenderness (no
guarding, no rebound), or CVA tenderness and soft and non-distended. Liver: non-tender and no hepatomegaly.
Spleen: non-tender and no splenomegaly. Hernia: none palpable.
Musculoskeletal:: Musculoskeletal: no contractures, tenderness, or bony abnormalities and normal range of
Neurologic: Gait and Station: shuffling. Cranial Nerves: grossly intact. Motor Strength and Tone: normal
strength and tone. Sensation: grossly intact. Reflexes: normal DTRs throughout. Coordination and Cerebellum:
Skin: Inspection and palpation: no rash, lacerations, scars, lesions, ulcers, induration, nodules, or jaundice and
Lymphatic: Cervical: no enlargement or tenderness. Supraclavicular: no enlargement or tenderness. Inguinal:
Psychiatric: Insight: good judgement. Mental Status: normal mood and affect and active and alert. Orientation:
to time, place, and person. Memory: recent memory normal and remote memory normal.
Worsening. Recent blood work within normal limits. Neurological exam negative. Updated MRI 3/4/2025
demonstrates chronic small vessel ischemic changes. S/P SLP. Followed by neurology. DAT Scan negative.
As above. However, the hyperintense T2 change in periventricular and subcortical bilateral white matter
changes noted on MRI may also be a result of migraine headaches. Neurology to address. ASA 81 mg daily.
P-tau217 positive. However, neurology is more concerned for PSP as the diagnosis. On WAVi, her physical
reaction time is slow. Peak frequency is slow in all areas. Her P300 is slow as well. Findings most c/w a
neurodegenerative condition, such as Parkinson's disease or PSP.
Stable. Suspect autonomic dysfunction. CARTs confirms. Followed by cardiology.
G90.9: Disorder of the autonomic nervous system, unspecified
Stable. May be central, but has history of BPPV. Have referred to PT for balance therapy.
Stable. Continue fiber supplementation and adequate hydration. Followed by gastroenterology.
Stable. Advised fiber supplementation and hydration. Followed by gastroenterology.
